message: Jake
Ash
Welcome, thank you for your hard work this time.
That way of putting it kinda stings.
Hehe.
Well, anyway, it’s good that we made it out.
Welcome.
Look
Talk

As usual, the customers are sparse, and it doesn’t look like it’s bustling. Maybe the fact that it hasn't closed means it makes a decent profit?
Inside the bar, there aren’t many decorations. With it being this dark, there probably aren't many people who want to strain their eyes to see those things.
Due to the extremely plain appearance, rowdy and foolish young people don’t spoil the bar's atmosphere. That’s about the only good thing about this place.
As usual.
That is to say,
it’s a phrase I’ve used many times to describe it, but actually seeing him when he’s not just ordinary is rare.
It's been a while since I saw your face, so it feels oddly nostalgic. It was only a few days, though.
You don't seem like you are, but I wonder if you were a bit worried about me.
At one point, I really wondered what was going to happen.
I truly apologize for making you worry.
I was really worried.
Please don't worry so much. Losing an important customer reduces my income, and that's a hassle.
So, my wallet is more important than my life, huh?
Hey.
That's not what I meant to say.
But, that's not entirely untrue, is it?
Anyway, I'm glad you made it out safely.
Hey, you don't know where Guy's traveling to, do you?
Why would I know? I mean, it's not impossible to look it up, though.
That's true.
Of course, you wouldn't know.
Naturally. I’m not particularly interested in Guy's private life, you know.
Is Guy missing again?
I told you, he went on a trip with Kristy.
What? I'm stunned.
Are you planning to interfere with them?
I'm not looking to interfere, but there's something I want to confirm.
No, I have something I want to talk about.
Should we look up where the two are headed?
No, it's fine.
Not right now.
It's fine.
In that case, I won't do anything.
Let me know if you need anything.
If you ask, I can look it up anytime.
Guy actually didn't tell Nets where he was going.
So, what's your point?
No, there's no way he's actually running away abroad or anything.
Not really.
Are you suspicious of Guy?
Right now, I feel like many people seem suspicious.
While it might be unavoidable to be paranoid, isn't it pointless to just doubt aimlessly?
I've been sorting things out in the detention center. As a result, the ones I find suspicious are Kristy and Guy.
I think that's just theory. First of all, the killer might be connected to the incident that started in San Francisco.
You should calm down a bit.
You know, Kristy met with those women who were recently murdered.
But to suspect her of being the killer is hasty.
I get it. I understand, but...
I'm just really concerned.
I understand how you feel, but...
If you ask her directly, it might just be something that seems trivial.
I wonder, but I have a feeling it's rooted in resentment towards Guy. Almost all of it.
You shouldn't start off by being suspicious. It's not like you.
Why don't you trust Kristy?
Actually, you know, Günter once said that Kristy has the potential to commit murder.
So that's why you're suspecting her of being the killer?
Isn't it natural to think so? A psychiatrist said that, you know? I was shocked too, but there are just too many suspicious factors.
There are certainly many suspicions. However, I haven't even met her or talked to her directly.
Do you think I'm crazy for being suspicious in this situation?
Well...
Is it unacceptable to say that I can't say if it's crazy or not?
You can't understand. I'm feeling complicated too.
Maybe it's better to think about something else right now.
I wonder why they would think you're the culprit. You've got yourself in quite a mess.
You don't actually think that Guy and Kristy are the culprits, do you?
The more I think about it, the more it all fits together.
He must be tired; that's probably why he's thinking bad things.
When you're tired, you tend to think too pessimistically.
So there's no request right now, huh?
Yeah.
But still, I don't want to work for a while. I've got too much on my mind.
Well, there's that. Please take your time to rest.
Since there's no work to introduce at the moment, it might be nice to spend time doing what you like.
Yeah.
Fortunately, I can still make a living like this.
Seriously, that's quite an enviable position you have.
There's no need to force yourself to work.
You're still the kind of guy who doesn't show his emotions.
I bet you didn't even raise an eyebrow when you heard I was detained.
It seems like the shop didn't go under while I was detained. Maybe the back work is thriving?
If I were to ask, I could probably find out where Guy and Kristy are in no time. But,
the case isn't resolved yet, and I’m one of the suspects; how can they be so carefree to go on a trip.
To leave without even telling me where they’re going! It feels just like they’re running away.
I wonder when they'll come back.
Since Guy came back, he seems to have returned to a normal state of mind, but I can't tell if he's really recovered.
It's great that Meryl protected Guy with her knife, but it's hard to believe she's so accustomed to it that she feels no fear.
Günter's words are bothering me.
Has Kristy really killed someone?
What do they mean, that I'm the criminal? I don't think the police can arrest the real culprit.
Now that they know I'm not the criminal, the police have to decide on the next suspect. Who will they target next?
Anyway, I just hope there won't be more victims.
Gabriela canceled her request, and Meryl's situation turned out like that, but there's nothing more to do.
For now, there's nothing that can really be called work. However, we can't let the serial killer run free.
I sometimes want to take it easy, but...
That's not going to happen.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
